system.serviceAreaNotes: Cal Water East Los Angeles district system with a 2024 CCR showing treated-water PFHxS PFOA and PFOS averages as non-detect while the utility continues PFAS treatment planning for wells in the district

next_action.title: Keep monitoring utility updates; no immediate escalation from current normalized data
next_action.summary: The current normalized utility observations are below the selected benchmark, so there is no immediate escalation from this data alone.
next_action.scope_note: Public water utility interpretation; not a household tap test result.

why_this: This route is for a household on public water, so direct utility observations outrank ZIP hints, fear copy, and generic product pages.
Current normalized assessment counts: 0 above selected benchmark, 0 present below selected benchmark.
Certified point-of-use is treated as the first escalation class only after the utility record is interpretable.
what_this_does_not_tell_you: This is not a household tap test.
Future utility updates can change the picture.
Do not convert this into a blanket safe or unsafe claim.

notes: No normalized upfront cost is available for the current option set.
No normalized annual replacement cost is available for the current option set.

certification_checklist.label: Claim-level listing for the specific PFAS you care about
certification_checklist.detail: Verify the listing record names the PFAS reduction claim directly instead of inferring from a product category.

certification_checklist.label: Match the certification standard to the actual claim
certification_checklist.detail: Use NSF ANSI 53 or an equivalent claim-level listing only when the record explicitly carries the reduction claim.

certification_checklist.label: Check replacement cadence and cartridge capacity before buying
certification_checklist.detail: Cost and maintenance burden change materially if cadence is annual vs more frequent.

when_to_escalate: A current utility notice or updated direct result moves one or more contaminants above the selected benchmark.
The household still wants extra ingestion-focused margin after the utility route is understood and the maintenance burden feels proportionate.
Your household goal expands beyond drinking and cooking water, which requires a separate whole-house justification review rather than a default upgrade.

sources.title: East Los Angeles 2024 Water Quality Report
East Los Angeles District 2024 Water Quality Report

sources.organization: California State Water Resources Control Board
sources.title: Water System Details: California Water Service Co. - ELA

decision_input.summary: A direct utility document exists, so the engine should explain benchmark context and optional next steps before any certified filter comparison.
